etymology.description: Originally depicted a stork -> same as й›€ -> phonetic еђ… (cry) added early in history -> current form й›љ
etymology.interpretation: The character й›љ began as a pictogram resembling a stork. To convey both its phonetic properties and its meaning related to the cries of waterfowl, the component еђ… was incorporated early on as an additional phonetic marker.
etymology.simplification: Now written as йё›(й›љ) (guГ n, "stork") with йёџ(йіҐ) (niЗЋo, "bird") signifying a bird's cry.
